Chronic viral mimicry induction following p53 loss promotes immune evasion

evidence_entities.text: Epigenetic therapies facilitate transcription of immunogenic repetitive elements that cull cancer cells through 'viral mimicry' responses.
cancer-initiating events also facilitate transcription of repetitive elements
p53 loss permits transcription of immunogenic repetitive elements and chronic viral mimicry activation
viral mimicry conditioning following p53 loss promotes immune evasion
